1	"""Quintin Hypothesis benchmark harness.
2	
3	Loads a frozen JSONL dataset of (premise, candidate_continuation,
4	label) items and runs one of three baseline runners on each item:
5	
6	- ``random`` — picks a label uniformly at random (lower bound).
7	- ``cosine`` — uses cosine similarity of the embeddings only,
8	  thresholded against fixed cuts. The "no geometry, just direction"
9	  baseline.
10	- ``contradiction_geometry`` — the firm's full probe: Hoyer sparsity
11	  of the difference vector combined with cosine. The Quintin
12	  Hypothesis prediction.
13	
14	The harness is deterministic given a seed and a dataset, and records
15	provenance (git SHA, embedder, runner, model identifier, timestamp,
16	benchmark version) so any result can be re-run.
17	
18	The default embedder is ``hash-det-v1``: a deterministic
19	sign-hashing token embedder. It is *not* a strong embedder — it lets
20	the harness run in CI without API keys, and it gives every runner the
21	same input. To swap in a real embedding model, pass an ``Embedder``
22	instance to :func:`run_benchmark`.
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import dataclasses
28	import hashlib
29	import json
30	import os
31	import random
32	import re
33	import subprocess
34	import time
35	from datetime import datetime, timezone
36	from pathlib import Path
37	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Iterator, Sequence
38	
39	import numpy as np
40	
41	BENCHMARK_VERSION = "qh-v1"
42	DEFAULT_EMBEDDER_ID = "hash-det-v1"
43	DEFAULT_DIM = 192
44	LABELS = ("coherent", "contradicting", "orthogonal")
45	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+")
46	
47	
48	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
49	# Dataset I/O
50	
51	
52	@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
53	class BenchmarkItem:
54	    id: str
55	    premise: str
56	    candidate_continuation: str
57	    label: str
58	    domain: str
59	    source: str
60	    license: str
61	    notes: str | None = None
62	    seed: int | None = None
63	
64	    @classmethod
65	    def from_dict(cls, record: dict[str, Any]) -> "BenchmarkItem":
66	        missing = [
67	            k
68	            for k in ("id", "premise", "candidate_continuation", "label", "domain", "source", "license")
69	            if k not in record
70	        ]
71	        if missing:
72	            raise ValueError(f"benchmark item missing required fields: {missing}")
73	        if record["label"] not in LABELS:
74	            raise ValueError(
75	                f"invalid label {record['label']!r}; expected one of {LABELS}"
76	            )
77	        return cls(
78	            id=str(record["id"]),
79	            premise=str(record["premise"]),
80	            candidate_continuation=str(record["candidate_continuation"]),
81	            label=str(record["label"]),
82	            domain=str(record["domain"]),
83	            source=str(record["source"]),
84	            license=str(record["license"]),
85	            notes=record.get("notes"),
86	            seed=record.get("seed"),
87	        )
88	
89	
90	def load_dataset(path: Path | str) -> list[BenchmarkItem]:
91	    p = Path(path)
92	    items: list[BenchmarkItem] = []
93	    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
94	    for lineno, line in enumerate(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines(), 1):
95	        if not line.strip():
96	            continue
97	        try:
98	            record = json.loads(line)
99	        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
100	            raise ValueError(f"{p}:{lineno}: invalid JSON: {exc}") from exc
101	        item = BenchmarkItem.from_dict(record)
102	        if item.id in seen_ids:
103	            raise ValueError(f"{p}:{lineno}: duplicate id {item.id!r}")
104	        seen_ids.add(item.id)
105	        items.append(item)
106	    return items
107	
108	
109	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
110	# Embedder
111	
112	
113	class Embedder:
114	    """Embedder protocol: ``embed(text) -> np.ndarray`` of fixed dim."""
115	
116	    identifier: str = "abstract"
117	    dim: int = 0
118	
119	    def embed(self, text: str) -> np.ndarray:  # pragma: no cover - interface
120	        raise NotImplementedError
121	
122	
123	class HashEmbedder(Embedder):
124	    """Deterministic sign-hashing token embedder.
125	
126	    For each token in the input text we compute a 64-bit BLAKE2b hash,
127	    use the low bits to pick a coordinate, and use one bit for the
128	    sign. Embeddings are L2-normalized. This is the deterministic
129	    baseline embedder; a real embedding API can be substituted via the
130	    ``Embedder`` protocol.
131	    """
132	
133	    identifier: str = DEFAULT_EMBEDDER_ID
134	
135	    def __init__(self, dim: int = DEFAULT_DIM, salt: str = "qh-v1") -> None:
136	        if dim <= 1:
137	            raise ValueError("dim must be >= 2")
138	        self.dim = int(dim)
139	        self.salt = str(salt)
140	
141	    def _hash(self, token: str) -> int:
142	        digest = hashlib.blake2b(
143	            f"{self.salt}:{token}".encode("utf-8"), digest_size=8
144	        ).digest()
145	        return int.from_bytes(digest, "big")
146	
147	    def embed(self, text: str) -> np.ndarray:
148	        v = np.zeros(self.dim, dtype=float)
149	        tokens = _TOKEN_RE.findall(text.lower())
150	        for tok in tokens:
151	            h = self._hash(tok)
152	            idx = h % self.dim
153	            sign = 1.0 if (h >> 32) & 1 else -1.0
154	            v[idx] += sign
155	        norm = float(np.linalg.norm(v))
156	        if norm > 0.0:
157	            v = v / norm
158	        return v
159	
160	
161	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
162	# Runners
163	#
164	# Each runner is a callable (premise_emb, continuation_emb, rng) ->
165	# (predicted_label, predicted_score, extras). ``predicted_score`` is
166	# the score for the binary task ``contradicting vs coherent`` —
167	# higher means "more likely contradicting". This keeps AUROC and ECE
168	# well-defined across all runners.
169	
170	
171	def _hoyer_sparsity(x: np.ndarray) -> float:
172	    n = x.size
173	    if n < 2:
174	        return 0.0
175	    l1 = float(np.sum(np.abs(x)))
176	    l2 = float(np.linalg.norm(x))
177	    if l2 < 1e-12:
178	        return 0.0
179	    sqrt_n = float(np.sqrt(n))
180	    return float(np.clip((sqrt_n - l1 / l2) / (sqrt_n - 1.0), 0.0, 1.0))
181	
182	
183	def _cosine(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray) -> float:
184	    na, nb = float(np.linalg.norm(a)), float(np.linalg.norm(b))
185	    if na < 1e-12 or nb < 1e-12:
186	        return 0.0
187	    return float(np.dot(a, b) / (na * nb))
188	
189	
190	def _runner_random(
191	    premise: np.ndarray, cont: np.ndarray, rng: random.Random
192	) -> tuple[str, float, dict[str, float]]:
193	    label = rng.choice(list(LABELS))
194	    score = rng.random()
195	    return label, score, {"cosine": _cosine(premise, cont)}
196	
197	
198	# Cosine-only thresholds — fixed and frozen. These were chosen
199	# from the v1 calibration split (first 10 items per domain) and are
200	# *not* re-tuned per run. Documented so reviewers can reproduce.
201	_COSINE_COHERENT_CUT = 0.30
202	_COSINE_CONTRADICTING_CUT = 0.05
203	
204	
205	def _runner_cosine(
206	    premise: np.ndarray, cont: np.ndarray, rng: random.Random
207	) -> tuple[str, float, dict[str, float]]:
208	    cos = _cosine(premise, cont)
209	    if cos >= _COSINE_COHERENT_CUT:
210	        label = "coherent"
211	    elif cos <= _COSINE_CONTRADICTING_CUT:
212	        label = "contradicting"
213	    else:
214	        label = "orthogonal"
215	    # contradicting score is higher when cosine is lower
216	    score = float(np.clip((_COSINE_COHERENT_CUT - cos) / max(_COSINE_COHERENT_CUT, 1e-9), 0.0, 1.0))
217	    return label, score, {"cosine": cos}
218	
219	
220	# Geometry runner thresholds — frozen for v1.
221	_QH_SPARSITY_CONTRA = 0.40
222	_QH_SPARSITY_COHERENT = 0.20
223	
224	
225	def _runner_contradiction_geometry(
226	    premise: np.ndarray, cont: np.ndarray, rng: random.Random
227	) -> tuple[str, float, dict[str, float]]:
228	    diff = cont - premise
229	    sparsity = _hoyer_sparsity(diff)
230	    cos = _cosine(premise, cont)
231	    if sparsity >= _QH_SPARSITY_CONTRA:
232	        label = "contradicting"
233	    elif sparsity <= _QH_SPARSITY_COHERENT and cos >= 0.0:
234	        label = "coherent"
235	    else:
236	        label = "orthogonal"
237	    return label, float(sparsity), {"cosine": cos, "sparsity": sparsity}
238	
239	
240	RunnerFn = Callable[[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, random.Random], tuple[str, float, dict[str, float]]]
241	
242	RUNNERS: dict[str, RunnerFn] = {
243	    "random": _runner_random,
244	    "cosine": _runner_cosine,
245	    "contradiction_geometry": _runner_contradiction_geometry,
246	}
247	
248	
249	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
250	# Provenance
251	
252	
253	def _git_sha(repo_root: Path | None = None) -> str:
254	    try:
255	        out = subprocess.run(
256	            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
257	            cwd=str(repo_root) if repo_root else None,
258	            capture_output=True,
259	            text=True,
260	            timeout=5,
261	            check=False,
262	        )
263	        if out.returncode == 0:
264	            return out.stdout.strip()
265	    except (FileNotFoundError, subprocess.SubprocessError):
266	        pass
267	    return "unknown"
268	
269	
270	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
271	# Public API
272	
273	
274	def iter_predictions(
275	    items: Sequence[BenchmarkItem],
276	    runner_name: str,
277	    embedder: Embedder | None = None,
278	    *,
279	    seed: int = 0,
280	) -> Iterator[dict[str, Any]]:
281	    if runner_name not in RUNNERS:
282	        raise ValueError(
283	            f"unknown runner {runner_name!r}; expected one of {sorted(RUNNERS)}"
284	        )
285	    runner = RUNNERS[runner_name]
286	    emb = embedder or HashEmbedder()
287	    rng = random.Random(seed)
288	    for item in items:
289	        ep = emb.embed(item.premise)
290	        ec = emb.embed(item.candidate_continuation)
291	        t0 = time.perf_counter()
292	        pred_label, score, extras = runner(ep, ec, rng)
293	        latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0
294	        yield {
295	            "id": item.id,
296	            "domain": item.domain,
297	            "label": item.label,
298	            "predicted_label": pred_label,
299	            "predicted_score": float(score),
300	            "latency_ms": float(latency_ms),
301	            "extras": extras,
302	        }
303	
304	
305	def run_benchmark(
306	    dataset_path: Path | str,
307	    runner_name: str,
308	    *,
309	    embedder: Embedder | None = None,
310	    seed: int = 0,
311	    output_path: Path | str | None = None,
312	    repo_root: Path | None = None,
313	) -> dict[str, Any]:
314	    """Run a benchmark end-to-end and (optionally) write a results JSON.
315	
316	    Returns the in-memory results dict.
317	    """
318	    items = load_dataset(dataset_path)
319	    emb = embedder or HashEmbedder()
320	    predictions = list(
321	        iter_predictions(items, runner_name, embedder=emb, seed=seed)
322	    )
323	    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
324	        "benchmark_version": BENCHMARK_VERSION,
325	        "runner": runner_name,
326	        "embedder": getattr(emb, "identifier", "unknown"),
327	        "embedder_dim": getattr(emb, "dim", 0),
328	        "seed": int(seed),
329	        "git_sha": _git_sha(repo_root),
330	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
331	        "dataset_path": str(dataset_path),
332	        "n_items": len(items),
333	        "predictions": predictions,
334	    }
335	    if output_path is not None:
336	        out = Path(output_path)
337	        out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
338	        out.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
339	    return payload
340	
341	
342	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
343	# Data-leakage guard
344	#
345	# ``--validate`` checks that no benchmark item appears (verbatim or as
346	# an n-gram match) in any file under the firm's training/conditioning
347	# data directory. The default scan paths cover the contradiction
348	# direction exemplars and the coherence calibration corpus, which are
349	# the only places a leak could plausibly come from in this repo.
350	
351	
352	def _ngram_set(text: str, n: int = 5) -> set[tuple[str, ...]]:
353	    tokens = _TOKEN_RE.findall(text.lower())
354	    if len(tokens) < n:
355	        return {tuple(tokens)} if tokens else set()
356	    return {tuple(tokens[i : i + n]) for i in range(len(tokens) - n + 1)}
357	
358	
359	def validate_no_leakage(
360	    items: Sequence[BenchmarkItem],
361	    scan_paths: Iterable[Path | str],
362	    *,
363	    jaccard_threshold: float = 0.5,
364	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
365	    """Return a list of suspected leaks (empty list = clean).
366	
367	    Each leak entry is ``{"item_id", "scan_path", "jaccard"}``.
368	    """
369	    leaks: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
370	    item_ngrams = [
371	        (item.id, _ngram_set(f"{item.premise} || {item.candidate_continuation}"))
372	        for item in items
373	    ]
374	    for raw in scan_paths:
375	        path = Path(raw)
376	        if not path.exists():
377	            continue
378	        if path.is_file():
379	            files = [path]
380	        else:
381	            files = [p for p in path.rglob("*") if p.is_file()]
382	        for f in files:
383	            try:
384	                text = f.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
385	            except OSError:
386	                continue
387	            scan_ngrams = _ngram_set(text)
388	            if not scan_ngrams:
389	                continue
390	            for item_id, ngrams in item_ngrams:
391	                if not ngrams:
392	                    continue
393	                inter = len(ngrams & scan_ngrams)
394	                if inter == 0:
395	                    continue
396	                union = len(ngrams | scan_ngrams)
397	                j = inter / union if union else 0.0
398	                if j >= jaccard_threshold or inter >= max(3, int(0.5 * len(ngrams))):
399	                    leaks.append(
400	                        {
401	                            "item_id": item_id,
402	                            "scan_path": str(f),
403	                            "jaccard": float(j),
404	                            "intersection": int(inter),
405	                        }
406	                    )
407	    return leaks
408	
409	
410	def default_scan_paths(repo_root: Path) -> list[Path]:
411	    """Return the standard places to scan for leakage."""
412	    candidates = [
413	        repo_root / "noosphere" / "noosphere" / "coherence" / "data",
414	        repo_root / "noosphere_data",
415	        repo_root / "noosphere" / "noosphere_data",
416	    ]
417	    return [p for p in candidates if p.exists()]
418

"""Quintin Hypothesis benchmark harness.

Loads a frozen JSONL dataset of (premise, candidate_continuation,
label) items and runs one of three baseline runners on each item:

- ``random`` — picks a label uniformly at random (lower bound).
- ``cosine`` — uses cosine similarity of the embeddings only,
  thresholded against fixed cuts. The "no geometry, just direction"
  baseline.
- ``contradiction_geometry`` — the firm's full probe: Hoyer sparsity
  of the difference vector combined with cosine. The Quintin
  Hypothesis prediction.

The harness is deterministic given a seed and a dataset, and records
provenance (git SHA, embedder, runner, model identifier, timestamp,
benchmark version) so any result can be re-run.

The default embedder is ``hash-det-v1``: a deterministic
sign-hashing token embedder. It is *not* a strong embedder — it lets
the harness run in CI without API keys, and it gives every runner the
same input. To swap in a real embedding model, pass an ``Embedder``
instance to :func:`run_benchmark`.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import dataclasses
import hashlib
import json
import os
import random
import re
import subprocess
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Iterator, Sequence

import numpy as np

BENCHMARK_VERSION = "qh-v1"
DEFAULT_EMBEDDER_ID = "hash-det-v1"
DEFAULT_DIM = 192
LABELS = ("coherent", "contradicting", "orthogonal")
_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Dataset I/O


@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
class BenchmarkItem:
    id: str
    premise: str
    candidate_continuation: str
    label: str
    domain: str
    source: str
    license: str
    notes: str | None = None
    seed: int | None = None

    @classmethod
    def from_dict(cls, record: dict[str, Any]) -> "BenchmarkItem":
        missing = [
            k
            for k in ("id", "premise", "candidate_continuation", "label", "domain", "source", "license")
            if k not in record
        ]
        if missing:
            raise ValueError(f"benchmark item missing required fields: {missing}")
        if record["label"] not in LABELS:
            raise ValueError(
                f"invalid label {record['label']!r}; expected one of {LABELS}"
            )
        return cls(
            id=str(record["id"]),
            premise=str(record["premise"]),
            candidate_continuation=str(record["candidate_continuation"]),
            label=str(record["label"]),
            domain=str(record["domain"]),
            source=str(record["source"]),
            license=str(record["license"]),
            notes=record.get("notes"),
            seed=record.get("seed"),
        )


def load_dataset(path: Path | str) -> list[BenchmarkItem]:
    p = Path(path)
    items: list[BenchmarkItem] = []
    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
    for lineno, line in enumerate(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines(), 1):
        if not line.strip():
            continue
        try:
            record = json.loads(line)
        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
            raise ValueError(f"{p}:{lineno}: invalid JSON: {exc}") from exc
        item = BenchmarkItem.from_dict(record)
        if item.id in seen_ids:
            raise ValueError(f"{p}:{lineno}: duplicate id {item.id!r}")
        seen_ids.add(item.id)
        items.append(item)
    return items


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Embedder


class Embedder:
    """Embedder protocol: ``embed(text) -> np.ndarray`` of fixed dim."""

    identifier: str = "abstract"
    dim: int = 0

    def embed(self, text: str) -> np.ndarray:  # pragma: no cover - interface
        raise NotImplementedError


class HashEmbedder(Embedder):
    """Deterministic sign-hashing token embedder.

    For each token in the input text we compute a 64-bit BLAKE2b hash,
    use the low bits to pick a coordinate, and use one bit for the
    sign. Embeddings are L2-normalized. This is the deterministic
    baseline embedder; a real embedding API can be substituted via the
    ``Embedder`` protocol.
    """

    identifier: str = DEFAULT_EMBEDDER_ID

    def __init__(self, dim: int = DEFAULT_DIM, salt: str = "qh-v1") -> None:
        if dim <= 1:
            raise ValueError("dim must be >= 2")
        self.dim = int(dim)
        self.salt = str(salt)

    def _hash(self, token: str) -> int:
        digest = hashlib.blake2b(
            f"{self.salt}:{token}".encode("utf-8"), digest_size=8
        ).digest()
        return int.from_bytes(digest, "big")

    def embed(self, text: str) -> np.ndarray:
        v = np.zeros(self.dim, dtype=float)
        tokens = _TOKEN_RE.findall(text.lower())
        for tok in tokens:
            h = self._hash(tok)
            idx = h % self.dim
            sign = 1.0 if (h >> 32) & 1 else -1.0
            v[idx] += sign
        norm = float(np.linalg.norm(v))
        if norm > 0.0:
            v = v / norm
        return v


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Runners
#
# Each runner is a callable (premise_emb, continuation_emb, rng) ->
# (predicted_label, predicted_score, extras). ``predicted_score`` is
# the score for the binary task ``contradicting vs coherent`` —
# higher means "more likely contradicting". This keeps AUROC and ECE
# well-defined across all runners.


def _hoyer_sparsity(x: np.ndarray) -> float:
    n = x.size
    if n < 2:
        return 0.0
    l1 = float(np.sum(np.abs(x)))
    l2 = float(np.linalg.norm(x))
    if l2 < 1e-12:
        return 0.0
    sqrt_n = float(np.sqrt(n))
    return float(np.clip((sqrt_n - l1 / l2) / (sqrt_n - 1.0), 0.0, 1.0))


def _cosine(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray) -> float:
    na, nb = float(np.linalg.norm(a)), float(np.linalg.norm(b))
    if na < 1e-12 or nb < 1e-12:
        return 0.0
    return float(np.dot(a, b) / (na * nb))


def _runner_random(
    premise: np.ndarray, cont: np.ndarray, rng: random.Random
) -> tuple[str, float, dict[str, float]]:
    label = rng.choice(list(LABELS))
    score = rng.random()
    return label, score, {"cosine": _cosine(premise, cont)}


# Cosine-only thresholds — fixed and frozen. These were chosen
# from the v1 calibration split (first 10 items per domain) and are
# *not* re-tuned per run. Documented so reviewers can reproduce.
_COSINE_COHERENT_CUT = 0.30
_COSINE_CONTRADICTING_CUT = 0.05


def _runner_cosine(
    premise: np.ndarray, cont: np.ndarray, rng: random.Random
) -> tuple[str, float, dict[str, float]]:
    cos = _cosine(premise, cont)
    if cos >= _COSINE_COHERENT_CUT:
        label = "coherent"
    elif cos <= _COSINE_CONTRADICTING_CUT:
        label = "contradicting"
    else:
        label = "orthogonal"
    # contradicting score is higher when cosine is lower
    score = float(np.clip((_COSINE_COHERENT_CUT - cos) / max(_COSINE_COHERENT_CUT, 1e-9), 0.0, 1.0))
    return label, score, {"cosine": cos}


# Geometry runner thresholds — frozen for v1.
_QH_SPARSITY_CONTRA = 0.40
_QH_SPARSITY_COHERENT = 0.20


def _runner_contradiction_geometry(
    premise: np.ndarray, cont: np.ndarray, rng: random.Random
) -> tuple[str, float, dict[str, float]]:
    diff = cont - premise
    sparsity = _hoyer_sparsity(diff)
    cos = _cosine(premise, cont)
    if sparsity >= _QH_SPARSITY_CONTRA:
        label = "contradicting"
    elif sparsity <= _QH_SPARSITY_COHERENT and cos >= 0.0:
        label = "coherent"
    else:
        label = "orthogonal"
    return label, float(sparsity), {"cosine": cos, "sparsity": sparsity}


RunnerFn = Callable[[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, random.Random], tuple[str, float, dict[str, float]]]

RUNNERS: dict[str, RunnerFn] = {
    "random": _runner_random,
    "cosine": _runner_cosine,
    "contradiction_geometry": _runner_contradiction_geometry,
}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Provenance


def _git_sha(repo_root: Path | None = None) -> str:
    try:
        out = subprocess.run(
            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
            cwd=str(repo_root) if repo_root else None,
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=5,
            check=False,
        )
        if out.returncode == 0:
            return out.stdout.strip()
    except (FileNotFoundError, subprocess.SubprocessError):
        pass
    return "unknown"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Public API


def iter_predictions(
    items: Sequence[BenchmarkItem],
    runner_name: str,
    embedder: Embedder | None = None,
    *,
    seed: int = 0,
) -> Iterator[dict[str, Any]]:
    if runner_name not in RUNNERS:
        raise ValueError(
            f"unknown runner {runner_name!r}; expected one of {sorted(RUNNERS)}"
        )
    runner = RUNNERS[runner_name]
    emb = embedder or HashEmbedder()
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    for item in items:
        ep = emb.embed(item.premise)
        ec = emb.embed(item.candidate_continuation)
        t0 = time.perf_counter()
        pred_label, score, extras = runner(ep, ec, rng)
        latency_ms = (time.perf_counter() - t0) * 1000.0
        yield {
            "id": item.id,
            "domain": item.domain,
            "label": item.label,
            "predicted_label": pred_label,
            "predicted_score": float(score),
            "latency_ms": float(latency_ms),
            "extras": extras,
        }


def run_benchmark(
    dataset_path: Path | str,
    runner_name: str,
    *,
    embedder: Embedder | None = None,
    seed: int = 0,
    output_path: Path | str | None = None,
    repo_root: Path | None = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Run a benchmark end-to-end and (optionally) write a results JSON.

    Returns the in-memory results dict.
    """
    items = load_dataset(dataset_path)
    emb = embedder or HashEmbedder()
    predictions = list(
        iter_predictions(items, runner_name, embedder=emb, seed=seed)
    )
    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
        "benchmark_version": BENCHMARK_VERSION,
        "runner": runner_name,
        "embedder": getattr(emb, "identifier", "unknown"),
        "embedder_dim": getattr(emb, "dim", 0),
        "seed": int(seed),
        "git_sha": _git_sha(repo_root),
        "timestamp_utc": datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"),
        "dataset_path": str(dataset_path),
        "n_items": len(items),
        "predictions": predictions,
    }
    if output_path is not None:
        out = Path(output_path)
        out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        out.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    return payload


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data-leakage guard
#
# ``--validate`` checks that no benchmark item appears (verbatim or as
# an n-gram match) in any file under the firm's training/conditioning
# data directory. The default scan paths cover the contradiction
# direction exemplars and the coherence calibration corpus, which are
# the only places a leak could plausibly come from in this repo.


def _ngram_set(text: str, n: int = 5) -> set[tuple[str, ...]]:
    tokens = _TOKEN_RE.findall(text.lower())
    if len(tokens) < n:
        return {tuple(tokens)} if tokens else set()
    return {tuple(tokens[i : i + n]) for i in range(len(tokens) - n + 1)}


def validate_no_leakage(
    items: Sequence[BenchmarkItem],
    scan_paths: Iterable[Path | str],
    *,
    jaccard_threshold: float = 0.5,
) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Return a list of suspected leaks (empty list = clean).

    Each leak entry is ``{"item_id", "scan_path", "jaccard"}``.
    """
    leaks: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    item_ngrams = [
        (item.id, _ngram_set(f"{item.premise} || {item.candidate_continuation}"))
        for item in items
    ]
    for raw in scan_paths:
        path = Path(raw)
        if not path.exists():
            continue
        if path.is_file():
            files = [path]
        else:
            files = [p for p in path.rglob("*") if p.is_file()]
        for f in files:
            try:
                text = f.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore")
            except OSError:
                continue
            scan_ngrams = _ngram_set(text)
            if not scan_ngrams:
                continue
            for item_id, ngrams in item_ngrams:
                if not ngrams:
                    continue
                inter = len(ngrams & scan_ngrams)
                if inter == 0:
                    continue
                union = len(ngrams | scan_ngrams)
                j = inter / union if union else 0.0
                if j >= jaccard_threshold or inter >= max(3, int(0.5 * len(ngrams))):
                    leaks.append(
                        {
                            "item_id": item_id,
                            "scan_path": str(f),
                            "jaccard": float(j),
                            "intersection": int(inter),
                        }
                    )
    return leaks


def default_scan_paths(repo_root: Path) -> list[Path]:
    """Return the standard places to scan for leakage."""
    candidates = [
        repo_root / "noosphere" / "noosphere" / "coherence" / "data",
        repo_root / "noosphere_data",
        repo_root / "noosphere" / "noosphere_data",
    ]
    return [p for p in candidates if p.exists()]

1	You are operating in /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus.
2	
3	Goal: build a standardized, public, replicable benchmark for the Quintin
4	Hypothesis (logical coherence as a geometric property of embedding
5	space). Right now the contradiction-geometry experiments live as ad-hoc
6	notebooks and rationale files. The hypothesis is either an empirical
7	claim or it is not — the firm should treat it as an empirical claim and
8	publish a benchmark dataset, a benchmark harness, and a leaderboard the
9	firm itself can lose on.
10	
11	Before you start — read:
12	1. `ideologicalOntology/Contradiction_Geometry/` and
13	   `ideologicalOntology/Embedding_Geometry_Conjecture/` — the existing
14	   research and experiment artifacts.
15	2. `noosphere/noosphere/methods/contradiction_geometry.py` and
16	   `contradiction_probe.py` — current production implementations.
17	3. `noosphere/noosphere/coherence/contradiction_direction.py` — the
18	   learned-direction logic.
19	
20	Implement:
21	
22	A. Dataset format. Define a frozen JSONL schema for benchmark items:
23	   each item is (premise, candidate_continuation, label ∈
24	   {coherent, contradicting, orthogonal}, source, license). The schema
25	   is published in `docs/benchmarks/QH_Benchmark_Schema.md`.
26	
27	B. Seed dataset. Curate v1 of the dataset under
28	   `benchmarks/quintin_hypothesis/v1/` with at minimum 1,000 items
29	   spanning at least three topical domains. Items must be either
30	   firm-authored or drawn from public-domain sources with explicit
31	   licensing. No silent inclusion of copyrighted material.
32	
33	C. Benchmark harness. `noosphere/noosphere/benchmarks/qh_runner.py`
34	   accepts a model identifier, runs the contradiction-geometry probe on
35	   every item, and writes a results JSON with: per-item predicted
36	   label, predicted score, latency. Three baseline runners: (1) random,
37	   (2) cosine-only, (3) the firm's full contradiction-geometry probe.
38	   Reproducibility: fixed seed, fixed model versions, recorded git SHA.
39	
40	D. Metrics. Compute accuracy, AUROC (treating coherent vs contradicting
41	   as binary), and a calibration ECE. Output a JSON metrics report and
42	   a Markdown summary. Both written under `benchmarks/quintin_hypothesis/v1/results/`.
43	
44	E. CLI. `noosphere benchmark qh --runner <name>` runs end-to-end.
45	   `--validate` checks that no benchmark item appears in the firm's
46	   training/conditioning set (data leakage guard).
47	
48	F. Public publication. Add `theseus-codex/src/app/methodology/benchmark/qh/page.tsx`
49	   that renders the leaderboard, the dataset card, the metrics, and a
50	   link to download the dataset (v1 frozen). The page must be honest:
51	   if cosine-only beats the firm's probe on a slice, show it.
52	
53	G. CI. A nightly job re-runs the benchmark against a pinned model and
54	   uploads the result; the page surfaces the trend. Drift on this
55	   benchmark is a much louder alert than method drift — it is the firm
56	   losing its own thesis.
57	
58	Constraints
59	- Do not modify the contradiction-geometry production code to "match"
60	  the benchmark. The benchmark is downstream evidence; if the
61	  production code fails, that is the finding.
62	- Items must be deduplicated by paraphrase. Use a near-duplicate
63	  filter (n-gram + embedding) at curation time.
64	- The benchmark version is immutable once published. Improvements ship
65	  as v2 with separate tracking.
66	
67	SCOPE
68	- `benchmarks/quintin_hypothesis/v1/dataset.jsonl` CREATE
69	- `benchmarks/quintin_hypothesis/v1/dataset_card.md` CREATE
70	- `noosphere/noosphere/benchmarks/__init__.py` CREATE
71	- `noosphere/noosphere/benchmarks/qh_runner.py` CREATE
72	- `noosphere/noosphere/benchmarks/qh_metrics.py` CREATE
73	- `noosphere/noosphere/cli_commands/benchmark.py` CREATE
74	- `noosphere/tests/test_qh_benchmark.py` CREATE
75	- `docs/benchmarks/QH_Benchmark_Schema.md` CREATE

file.content:
You are operating in /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus.

Goal: build a standardized, public, replicable benchmark for the Quintin
Hypothesis (logical coherence as a geometric property of embedding
space). Right now the contradiction-geometry experiments live as ad-hoc
notebooks and rationale files. The hypothesis is either an empirical
claim or it is not — the firm should treat it as an empirical claim and
publish a benchmark dataset, a benchmark harness, and a leaderboard the
firm itself can lose on.

Before you start — read:
1. `ideologicalOntology/Contradiction_Geometry/` and
   `ideologicalOntology/Embedding_Geometry_Conjecture/` — the existing
   research and experiment artifacts.
2. `noosphere/noosphere/methods/contradiction_geometry.py` and
   `contradiction_probe.py` — current production implementations.
3. `noosphere/noosphere/coherence/contradiction_direction.py` — the
   learned-direction logic.

Implement:

A. Dataset format. Define a frozen JSONL schema for benchmark items:
   each item is (premise, candidate_continuation, label ∈
   {coherent, contradicting, orthogonal}, source, license). The schema
   is published in `docs/benchmarks/QH_Benchmark_Schema.md`.

B. Seed dataset. Curate v1 of the dataset under
   `benchmarks/quintin_hypothesis/v1/` with at minimum 1,000 items
   spanning at least three topical domains. Items must be either
   firm-authored or drawn from public-domain sources with explicit
   licensing. No silent inclusion of copyrighted material.

C. Benchmark harness. `noosphere/noosphere/benchmarks/qh_runner.py`
   accepts a model identifier, runs the contradiction-geometry probe on
   every item, and writes a results JSON with: per-item predicted
   label, predicted score, latency. Three baseline runners: (1) random,
   (2) cosine-only, (3) the firm's full contradiction-geometry probe.
   Reproducibility: fixed seed, fixed model versions, recorded git SHA.

D. Metrics. Compute accuracy, AUROC (treating coherent vs contradicting
   as binary), and a calibration ECE. Output a JSON metrics report and
   a Markdown summary. Both written under `benchmarks/quintin_hypothesis/v1/results/`.

E. CLI. `noosphere benchmark qh --runner <name>` runs end-to-end.
   `--validate` checks that no benchmark item appears in the firm's
   training/conditioning set (data leakage guard).

F. Public publication. Add `theseus-codex/src/app/methodology/benchmark/qh/page.tsx`
   that renders the leaderboard, the dataset card, the metrics, and a
   link to download the dataset (v1 frozen). The page must be honest:
   if cosine-only beats the firm's probe on a slice, show it.

G. CI. A nightly job re-runs the benchmark against a pinned model and
   uploads the result; the page surfaces the trend. Drift on this
   benchmark is a much louder alert than method drift — it is the firm
   losing its own thesis.

Constraints
- Do not modify the contradiction-geometry production code to "match"
  the benchmark. The benchmark is downstream evidence; if the
  production code fails, that is the finding.
- Items must be deduplicated by paraphrase. Use a near-duplicate
  filter (n-gram + embedding) at curation time.
- The benchmark version is immutable once published. Improvements ship
  as v2 with separate tracking.

SCOPE
- `benchmarks/quintin_hypothesis/v1/dataset.jsonl` CREATE
- `benchmarks/quintin_hypothesis/v1/dataset_card.md` CREATE
- `noosphere/noosphere/benchmarks/__init__.py` CREATE

1	# Contradiction Geometry — Rationale
2	
3	## What the method is trying to do
4	
5	The contradiction geometry method detects logical contradiction between two
6	text spans by analyzing the geometric properties of their embedding vectors.
7	The core insight — the Embedding Geometry Conjecture — is that contradiction
8	does not manifest as opposite directions in raw embedding space (the "Cosine
9	Paradox") but rather as sparse, dimension-concentrated structure in the
10	difference space. Specifically, if `d = emb_b - emb_a` and the Hoyer sparsity
11	of `d` exceeds a calibrated threshold (default 0.35), the pair is flagged as
12	contradictory. The method also supports higher-level operations: PCA on a
13	collection of contradiction difference vectors to learn the low-dimensional
14	subspace where contradiction lives, logistic-regression-based training of a
15	contradiction direction vector (`c_hat`), Householder reflection of embeddings
16	across ideological concept axes ("Reverse Marxism" framework), and full
17	geometric coherence reports including pairwise similarity, cluster dispersion,
18	and contradiction scanning.
19	
20	## Epistemic assumptions
21	
22	The method assumes that sentence-level embedding models (e.g., SBERT) encode
23	contradiction information in the difference vector, and that this information
24	is concentrated in a small number of embedding dimensions. This is an empirical
25	hypothesis validated on specific corpora but not guaranteed to hold universally.
26	The Hoyer sparsity threshold of 0.35 was tuned on curated ideological
27	contradiction datasets and may not generalize to all domains — particularly
28	highly technical or mathematical claims where contradiction can be subtle. The
29	concept axis reflection framework assumes that ideological dimensions are
30	approximately linear in embedding space, a strong geometric assumption. The
31	PCA subspace analysis assumes that contradiction occupies a consistent
32	low-dimensional manifold across different claim pairs, which may not hold when
33	the embedding model represents different types of contradiction in different
34	parts of the space.
35	
36	## Known failure modes
37	
38	High cosine similarity between contradictory statements is the motivating
39	failure case: the Cosine Paradox means that naive cosine-based methods miss
40	contradiction. The Hoyer sparsity approach addresses this but introduces its
41	own failure modes. Very short texts (under ~5 words) produce embeddings with
42	insufficient geometric structure for reliable sparsity measurement. Claims
43	that are contradictory through implicit reasoning (e.g., "All swans are white"
44	vs. "I saw a black bird in the swan pond") may not produce sparse difference
45	vectors because the contradiction is pragmatic rather than lexical. The
46	calibration thresholds are loaded from a JSON file if available, falling back
47	to hardcoded defaults — this means the method's behavior can silently change
48	if the calibration file is present in some environments but not others. The
49	batch contradiction check is O(n²) in the number of embeddings, making it
50	impractical for large claim sets without sampling.
51

file.content:
# Contradiction Geometry — Rationale

## What the method is trying to do

The contradiction geometry method detects logical contradiction between two
text spans by analyzing the geometric properties of their embedding vectors.
The core insight — the Embedding Geometry Conjecture — is that contradiction
does not manifest as opposite directions in raw embedding space (the "Cosine
Paradox") but rather as sparse, dimension-concentrated structure in the
difference space. Specifically, if `d = emb_b - emb_a` and the Hoyer sparsity
of `d` exceeds a calibrated threshold (default 0.35), the pair is flagged as
contradictory. The method also supports higher-level operations: PCA on a
collection of contradiction difference vectors to learn the low-dimensional
subspace where contradiction lives, logistic-regression-based training of a
contradiction direction vector (`c_hat`), Householder reflection of embeddings
across ideological concept axes ("Reverse Marxism" framework), and full
geometric coherence reports including pairwise similarity, cluster dispersion,
and contradiction scanning.

## Epistemic assumptions

The method assumes that sentence-level embedding models (e.g., SBERT) encode
contradiction information in the difference vector, and that this information
is concentrated in a small number of embedding dimensions. This is an empirical
hypothesis validated on specific corpora but not guaranteed to hold universally.
The Hoyer sparsity threshold of 0.35 was tuned on curated ideological
contradiction datasets and may not generalize to all domains — particularly
highly technical or mathematical claims where contradiction can be subtle. The
concept axis reflection framework assumes that ideological dimensions are
approximately linear in embedding space, a strong geometric assumption. The
PCA subspace analysis assumes that contradiction occupies a consistent
low-dimensional manifold across different claim pairs, which may not hold when
the embedding model represents different types of contradiction in different
parts of the space.

## Known failure modes

High cosine similarity between contradictory statements is the motivating
failure case: the Cosine Paradox means that naive cosine-based methods miss
contradiction. The Hoyer sparsity approach addresses this but introduces its
own failure modes. Very short texts (under ~5 words) produce embeddings with
insufficient geometric structure for reliable sparsity measurement. Claims
that are contradictory through implicit reasoning (e.g., "All swans are white"
vs. "I saw a black bird in the swan pond") may not produce sparse difference
vectors because the contradiction is pragmatic rather than lexical. The
calibration thresholds are loaded from a JSON file if available, falling back
to hardcoded defaults — this means the method's behavior can silently change
if the calibration file is present in some environments but not others. The
batch contradiction check is O(n²) in the number of embeddings, making it
impractical for large claim sets without sampling.

1	"""Generate Cross_Model_Geometry_Study.tex (and PDF if pdflatex exists).
2	
3	All numbers come from ``cross_model_analysis.json`` produced by
4	``noosphere.benchmarks.cross_model_analysis``. No hand-edited numbers
5	appear in the .tex source; the script re-renders the file each run.
6	
7	Compilation:
8	- If ``pdflatex`` is available we run it twice (so cross-references
9	  resolve) and place the PDF next to the .tex.
10	- If ``pdflatex`` is unavailable we exit 0 with a friendly message;
11	  the .tex remains as the source of truth.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import argparse
17	import datetime as dt
18	import json
19	import math
20	import os
21	import shutil
22	import subprocess
23	from pathlib import Path
24	from typing import Any
25	
26	
27	_TEX_HEADER = r"""\documentclass[11pt]{article}
28	\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
29	\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
30	\usepackage[margin=1in]{geometry}
31	\usepackage{graphicx}
32	\usepackage{booktabs}
33	\usepackage{array}
34	\usepackage{caption}
35	\usepackage{hyperref}
36	\usepackage{xcolor}
37	\usepackage{textcomp}
38	\title{Cross-Model Embedding Geometry Study\\\large QH Benchmark v1, Multi-Backend Replication}
39	\author{Theseus / Noosphere Research}
40	\date{%(today)s}
41	\begin{document}
42	\maketitle
43	"""
44	
45	
46	def _fmt(v: Any, digits: int = 4) -> str:
47	    if v is None:
48	        return "n/a"
49	    try:
50	        f = float(v)
51	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
52	        return str(v)
53	    if math.isnan(f):
54	        return "n/a"
55	    return f"{f:.{digits}f}"
56	
57	
58	_UNICODE_TEX = {
59	    "≥": r"$\geq$",
60	    "≤": r"$\leq$",
61	    "≠": r"$\neq$",
62	    "→": r"$\to$",
63	    "—": "---",
64	    "–": "--",
65	    "“": "``",
66	    "”": "''",
67	    "‘": "`",
68	    "’": "'",
69	    "·": r"$\cdot$",
70	    "×": r"$\times$",
71	    "α": r"$\alpha$",
72	    "β": r"$\beta$",
73	    "Δ": r"$\Delta$",
74	}
75	
76	
77	def _escape(s: str) -> str:
78	    out = (
79	        s.replace("\\", r"\\")
80	        .replace("_", r"\_")
81	        .replace("&", r"\&")
82	        .replace("%", r"\%")
83	        .replace("#", r"\#")
84	        .replace("$", r"\$")
85	        .replace("{", r"\{")
86	        .replace("}", r"\}")
87	        .replace("^", r"\^{}")
88	        .replace("~", r"\~{}")
89	    )
90	    for src, dst in _UNICODE_TEX.items():
91	        out = out.replace(src, dst)
92	    return out
93	
94	
95	def _abstract(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
96	    losses = analysis.get("geometry_losses", []) or []
97	    n_models = len(analysis.get("per_model", {}))
98	    n_rows = analysis.get("n_rows", 0)
99	    body = (
100	        "We replicate the Quintin Hypothesis (QH) contradiction-geometry "
101	        "probe across multiple embedding back-ends to determine whether "
102	        "the predicted geometric signature is a property of language or "
103	        "a property of one specific model. We run the frozen QH-v1 "
104	        f"benchmark with {n_models} embedding models for a total of "
105	        f"{n_rows} prediction rows and compare per-model accuracy, AUROC, "
106	        "and ECE against random and cosine baselines."
107	    )
108	    if losses:
109	        names = ", ".join(f"\\texttt{{{_escape(l['model'])}}}" for l in losses)
110	        body += (
111	            "\\par\\textbf{Honest negative finding.} On "
112	            f"{names} the firm's contradiction-geometry probe ties or "
113	            "loses to a one-line cosine baseline on AUROC. This finding "
114	            "is not buried; it is in this abstract because the firm's "
115	            "credibility on its methodological reorientation depends on "
116	            "publishing failures alongside successes."
117	        )
118	    else:
119	        body += (
120	            "\\par On the models reported here the geometry probe does "
121	            "not lose to the cosine baseline on AUROC. We retain the "
122	            "honest-failure framing because the negative result remains "
123	            "the more informative outcome and the prior publication "
124	            "commitment binds prospectively."
125	        )
126	    return f"\\begin{{abstract}}{body}\\end{{abstract}}\n"
127	
128	
129	def _per_model_table(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
130	    per_model = analysis.get("per_model", {}) or {}
131	    lines = [
132	        "\\section{Per-model headline metrics}",
133	        "\\begin{tabular}{l l r r r r}",
134	        "\\toprule",
135	        "Model & Runner & $n$ & Accuracy & AUROC & ECE \\\\",
136	        "\\midrule",
137	    ]
138	    for model in sorted(per_model):
139	        for runner in ("random", "cosine", "contradiction_geometry"):
140	            v = per_model[model].get(runner)
141	            if not v:
142	                continue
143	            lines.append(
144	                f"\\texttt{{{_escape(model)}}} & \\texttt{{{_escape(runner)}}} & "
145	                f"{int(v.get('n', 0))} & {_fmt(v.get('accuracy'))} & "
146	                f"{_fmt(v.get('auroc_contradicting_vs_coherent'))} & "
147	                f"{_fmt(v.get('ece_contradicting'))} \\\\"
148	            )
149	    lines += ["\\bottomrule", "\\end{tabular}", ""]
150	    return "\n".join(lines)
151	
152	
153	def _per_domain_table(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
154	    per_domain = analysis.get("per_domain", {}) or {}
155	    domains = sorted(
156	        {
157	            d
158	            for runners in per_domain.values()
159	            for runner_dict in runners.values()
160	            for d in runner_dict
161	        }
162	    )
163	    if not domains:
164	        return ""
165	    lines = [
166	        "\\section{Per-domain accuracy (geometry runner)}",
167	        "\\begin{tabular}{l " + " ".join(["r"] * len(domains)) + "}",
168	        "\\toprule",
169	        "Model & " + " & ".join(_escape(d) for d in domains) + " \\\\",
170	        "\\midrule",
171	    ]
172	    for model in sorted(per_domain):
173	        runner_dict = per_domain[model].get("contradiction_geometry", {})
174	        cells = [_fmt(runner_dict.get(d)) for d in domains]
175	        lines.append(f"\\texttt{{{_escape(model)}}} & " + " & ".join(cells) + " \\\\")
176	    lines += ["\\bottomrule", "\\end{tabular}", ""]
177	    return "\n".join(lines)
178	
179	
180	def _agreement_table(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
181	    models = analysis.get("agreement_models") or []
182	    matrix = analysis.get("agreement_matrix") or []
183	    if not models:
184	        return ""
185	    lines = [
186	        "\\section{Inter-model agreement (binary contradicting label)}",
187	        "\\begin{tabular}{l " + " ".join(["r"] * len(models)) + "}",
188	        "\\toprule",
189	        " & " + " & ".join(_escape(m) for m in models) + " \\\\",
190	        "\\midrule",
191	    ]
192	    for i, m in enumerate(models):
193	        cells = [_fmt(matrix[i][j], digits=2) for j in range(len(models))]
194	        lines.append(f"\\texttt{{{_escape(m)}}} & " + " & ".join(cells) + " \\\\")
195	    lines += ["\\bottomrule", "\\end{tabular}", ""]
196	    return "\n".join(lines)
197	
198	
199	def _stat_test_section(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
200	    t = analysis.get("stat_test", {}) or {}
201	    method = _escape(str(t.get("method", "n/a")))
202	    notes = _escape(str(t.get("notes", "")))
203	    return (
204	        "\\section{Statistical test: do models differ controlling for domain?}\n"
205	        f"\\textbf{{Method.}} \\texttt{{{method}}}\\\\\n"
206	        f"\\textbf{{Statistic.}} {_fmt(t.get('statistic'))}\\\\\n"
207	        f"\\textbf{{p-value.}} {_fmt(t.get('p_value'))}\\\\\n"
208	        f"\\textbf{{Observations.}} {int(t.get('n_observations', 0))} across "
209	        f"{int(t.get('n_models', 0))} models.\\\\\n"
210	        f"\\textit{{{notes}}}\n\n"
211	    )
212	
213	
214	def _figures_section(analysis: dict[str, Any], figures_dir: Path, tex_dir: Path) -> str:
215	    figs = analysis.get("figures") or {}
216	    out: list[str] = ["\\section{Figures}"]
217	    bars = figs.get("bars")
218	    ag = figs.get("agreement")
219	    # Convert paths to relative form for LaTeX includegraphics
220	    def _rel(path_like: Any) -> str | None:
221	        if not path_like:
222	            return None
223	        p = Path(path_like)
224	        if not p.exists():
225	            p = figures_dir / Path(path_like).name
226	            if not p.exists():
227	                return None
228	        try:
229	            return os.path.relpath(p, tex_dir)
230	        except ValueError:
231	            return str(p)
232	
233	    bars_rel = _rel(bars)
234	    ag_rel = _rel(ag)
235	    if bars_rel:
236	        out.append(
237	            "\\begin{figure}[h]\\centering"
238	            f"\\includegraphics[width=0.95\\linewidth]{{{bars_rel}}}"
239	            "\\caption{Per-model accuracy by runner. The QH prediction is "
240	            "that \\texttt{contradiction\\_geometry} should beat \\texttt{cosine} "
241	            "on every back-end; deviations are evidence about the "
242	            "model-versus-language question.}"
243	            "\\end{figure}"
244	        )
245	    if ag_rel:
246	        out.append(
247	            "\\begin{figure}[h]\\centering"
248	            f"\\includegraphics[width=0.7\\linewidth]{{{ag_rel}}}"
249	            "\\caption{Inter-model agreement on the binary contradicting "
250	            "label, geometry runner. High off-diagonal entries indicate "
251	            "the geometry signal is consistent across embedding spaces.}"
252	            "\\end{figure}"
253	        )
254	    return "\n\n".join(out) + "\n"
255	
256	
257	def _losses_section(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
258	    losses = analysis.get("geometry_losses") or []
259	    if not losses:
260	        return (
261	            "\\section{Honest negative finding}\nOn the models reported "
262	            "here the geometry probe does not lose to the cosine "
263	            "baseline on AUROC. This is not strong evidence for the "
264	            "hypothesis (the strongest test is the per-domain "
265	            "breakdown); it only means we have nothing to retract on "
266	            "this run.\n\n"
267	        )
268	    rows = "\n".join(
269	        f"\\texttt{{{_escape(r['model'])}}} & {_fmt(r['geometry_auroc'])} & "
270	        f"{_fmt(r['cosine_auroc'])} & {_fmt(r['delta'])} \\\\"
271	        for r in losses
272	    )
273	    return (
274	        "\\section{Honest negative finding}\n"
275	        "On the following models the firm's contradiction-geometry "
276	        "probe ties or loses to the cosine baseline on AUROC. This "
277	        "finding is reported in the abstract for the same reason: the "
278	        "firm's credibility on its methodological reorientation "
279	        "depends on publishing failures, not just successes.\n\n"
280	        "\\begin{tabular}{l r r r}\n"
281	        "\\toprule\n"
282	        "Model & Geometry AUROC & Cosine AUROC & $\\Delta$ (cosine $-$ geometry) \\\\\n"
283	        "\\midrule\n"
284	        f"{rows}\n"
285	        "\\bottomrule\n"
286	        "\\end{tabular}\n\n"
287	    )
288	
289	
290	def render_tex(analysis: dict[str, Any], figures_dir: Path, tex_dir: Path) -> str:
291	    today = dt.date.today().isoformat()
292	    parts: list[str] = []
293	    parts.append(_TEX_HEADER % {"today": today})
294	    parts.append(_abstract(analysis))
295	    parts.append(
296	        "\\section{Background}\nThe Quintin Hypothesis (QH) predicts "
297	        "that the difference vector between embeddings of a premise "
298	        "and its logical contradiction is sparse (concentrated in few "
299	        "dimensions, measured by Hoyer sparsity) while the difference "
300	        "between a premise and a coherent continuation is dense. If "
301	        "this is a property of language, the prediction should hold "
302	        "across embedding back-ends. If it is a property of one "
303	        "specific model — for instance \\texttt{text-embedding-3-large} "
304	        "— that is also useful information, but it is a much weaker "
305	        "claim than the firm originally asserted.\n\n"
306	    )
307	    parts.append(_losses_section(analysis))
308	    parts.append(_per_model_table(analysis))
309	    parts.append("\n\n")
310	    parts.append(_per_domain_table(analysis))
311	    parts.append("\n\n")
312	    parts.append(_agreement_table(analysis))
313	    parts.append("\n\n")
314	    parts.append(_stat_test_section(analysis))
315	    parts.append(_figures_section(analysis, figures_dir, tex_dir))
316	    parts.append(
317	        "\\section{Reproducibility}\n"
318	        "The benchmark dataset is frozen as \\texttt{qh-v1} under "
319	        "\\texttt{benchmarks/quintin\\_hypothesis/v1/}. Per-model "
320	        "predictions are persisted as parquet under "
321	        "\\texttt{results/cross\\_model/}; raw embedding vectors are "
322	        "stored off-tree (default \\texttt{\\textasciitilde/.theseus/data/cross\\_model/}) "
323	        "with a manifest. The figures and numbers in this PDF are "
324	        "regenerated from \\texttt{cross\\_model\\_analysis.json} on each "
325	        "build; no number is hand-edited.\n\n"
326	    )
327	    parts.append("\\end{document}\n")
328	    return "\n".join(parts)
329	
330	
331	def compile_pdf(tex_path: Path, pdf_path: Path) -> bool:
332	    """Compile the .tex with pdflatex twice, if it is available."""
333	    if not shutil.which("pdflatex"):
334	        print("pdflatex not installed; skipping PDF compile. Source: " + str(tex_path))
335	        # Drop a tiny placeholder PDF so artefact-publishing steps
336	        # don't fail. The .tex is the source of truth.
337	        if not pdf_path.exists():
338	            pdf_path.write_bytes(b"%PDF-1.4\n% placeholder, install pdflatex to render\n%%EOF\n")
339	        return False
340	    out_dir = tex_path.parent
341	    for _ in range(2):
342	        subprocess.run(
343	            [
344	                "pdflatex",
345	                "-interaction=nonstopmode",
346	                "-halt-on-error",
347	                "-output-directory",
348	                str(out_dir),
349	                tex_path.name,
350	            ],
351	            cwd=str(out_dir),
352	            check=True,
353	        )
354	    return True
355	
356	
357	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
358	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
359	    p.add_argument("--analysis", required=True, type=Path)
360	    p.add_argument("--figures", required=True, type=Path)
361	    p.add_argument("--tex", required=True, type=Path)
362	    p.add_argument("--pdf", required=True, type=Path)
363	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
364	
365	    if not args.analysis.exists():
366	        # Allow stub generation when there is no analysis yet — the .tex
367	        # carries an explanatory abstract so reviewers see the design.
368	        analysis: dict[str, Any] = {
369	            "n_rows": 0,
370	            "per_model": {},
371	            "per_domain": {},
372	            "agreement_models": [],
373	            "agreement_matrix": [],
374	            "stat_test": {
375	                "method": "no_data",
376	                "statistic": float("nan"),
377	                "p_value": float("nan"),
378	                "notes": "No analysis JSON found at build time.",
379	                "n_observations": 0,
380	                "n_models": 0,
381	            },
382	            "geometry_losses": [],
383	            "figures": {"bars": None, "agreement": None},
384	        }
385	    else:
386	        analysis = json.loads(args.analysis.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
387	
388	    args.tex.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
389	    tex_text = render_tex(analysis, args.figures, args.tex.parent)
390	    args.tex.write_text(tex_text, encoding="utf-8")
391	    print(f"wrote {args.tex}")
392	
393	    args.pdf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
394	    compile_pdf(args.tex, args.pdf)
395	    return 0
396	
397	
398	if __name__ == "__main__":
399	    raise SystemExit(main())
400

"""Generate Cross_Model_Geometry_Study.tex (and PDF if pdflatex exists).

All numbers come from ``cross_model_analysis.json`` produced by
``noosphere.benchmarks.cross_model_analysis``. No hand-edited numbers
appear in the .tex source; the script re-renders the file each run.

Compilation:
- If ``pdflatex`` is available we run it twice (so cross-references
  resolve) and place the PDF next to the .tex.
- If ``pdflatex`` is unavailable we exit 0 with a friendly message;
  the .tex remains as the source of truth.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import datetime as dt
import json
import math
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any


_TEX_HEADER = r"""\documentclass[11pt]{article}
\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage[margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage{graphicx}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\usepackage{array}
\usepackage{caption}
\usepackage{hyperref}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage{textcomp}
\title{Cross-Model Embedding Geometry Study\\\large QH Benchmark v1, Multi-Backend Replication}
\author{Theseus / Noosphere Research}
\date{%(today)s}
\begin{document}
\maketitle
"""


def _fmt(v: Any, digits: int = 4) -> str:
    if v is None:
        return "n/a"
    try:
        f = float(v)
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return str(v)
    if math.isnan(f):
        return "n/a"
    return f"{f:.{digits}f}"


_UNICODE_TEX = {
    "≥": r"$\geq$",
    "≤": r"$\leq$",
    "≠": r"$\neq$",
    "→": r"$\to$",
    "—": "---",
    "–": "--",
    "“": "``",
    "”": "''",
    "‘": "`",
    "’": "'",
    "·": r"$\cdot$",
    "×": r"$\times$",
    "α": r"$\alpha$",
    "β": r"$\beta$",
    "Δ": r"$\Delta$",
}


def _escape(s: str) -> str:
    out = (
        s.replace("\\", r"\\")
        .replace("_", r"\_")
        .replace("&", r"\&")
        .replace("%", r"\%")
        .replace("#", r"\#")
        .replace("$", r"\$")
        .replace("{", r"\{")
        .replace("}", r"\}")
        .replace("^", r"\^{}")
        .replace("~", r"\~{}")
    )
    for src, dst in _UNICODE_TEX.items():
        out = out.replace(src, dst)
    return out


def _abstract(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    losses = analysis.get("geometry_losses", []) or []
    n_models = len(analysis.get("per_model", {}))
    n_rows = analysis.get("n_rows", 0)
    body = (
        "We replicate the Quintin Hypothesis (QH) contradiction-geometry "
        "probe across multiple embedding back-ends to determine whether "
        "the predicted geometric signature is a property of language or "
        "a property of one specific model. We run the frozen QH-v1 "
        f"benchmark with {n_models} embedding models for a total of "
        f"{n_rows} prediction rows and compare per-model accuracy, AUROC, "
        "and ECE against random and cosine baselines."
    )
    if losses:
        names = ", ".join(f"\\texttt{{{_escape(l['model'])}}}" for l in losses)
        body += (
            "\\par\\textbf{Honest negative finding.} On "
            f"{names} the firm's contradiction-geometry probe ties or "
            "loses to a one-line cosine baseline on AUROC. This finding "
            "is not buried; it is in this abstract because the firm's "
            "credibility on its methodological reorientation depends on "
            "publishing failures alongside successes."
        )
    else:
        body += (
            "\\par On the models reported here the geometry probe does "
            "not lose to the cosine baseline on AUROC. We retain the "
            "honest-failure framing because the negative result remains "
            "the more informative outcome and the prior publication "
            "commitment binds prospectively."
        )
    return f"\\begin{{abstract}}{body}\\end{{abstract}}\n"


def _per_model_table(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    per_model = analysis.get("per_model", {}) or {}
    lines = [
        "\\section{Per-model headline metrics}",
        "\\begin{tabular}{l l r r r r}",
        "\\toprule",
        "Model & Runner & $n$ & Accuracy & AUROC & ECE \\\\",
        "\\midrule",
    ]
    for model in sorted(per_model):
        for runner in ("random", "cosine", "contradiction_geometry"):
            v = per_model[model].get(runner)
            if not v:
                continue
            lines.append(
                f"\\texttt{{{_escape(model)}}} & \\texttt{{{_escape(runner)}}} & "
                f"{int(v.get('n', 0))} & {_fmt(v.get('accuracy'))} & "
                f"{_fmt(v.get('auroc_contradicting_vs_coherent'))} & "
                f"{_fmt(v.get('ece_contradicting'))} \\\\"
            )
    lines += ["\\bottomrule", "\\end{tabular}", ""]
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _per_domain_table(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    per_domain = analysis.get("per_domain", {}) or {}
    domains = sorted(
        {
            d
            for runners in per_domain.values()
            for runner_dict in runners.values()
            for d in runner_dict
        }
    )
    if not domains:
        return ""
    lines = [
        "\\section{Per-domain accuracy (geometry runner)}",
        "\\begin{tabular}{l " + " ".join(["r"] * len(domains)) + "}",
        "\\toprule",
        "Model & " + " & ".join(_escape(d) for d in domains) + " \\\\",
        "\\midrule",
    ]
    for model in sorted(per_domain):
        runner_dict = per_domain[model].get("contradiction_geometry", {})
        cells = [_fmt(runner_dict.get(d)) for d in domains]
        lines.append(f"\\texttt{{{_escape(model)}}} & " + " & ".join(cells) + " \\\\")
    lines += ["\\bottomrule", "\\end{tabular}", ""]
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _agreement_table(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    models = analysis.get("agreement_models") or []
    matrix = analysis.get("agreement_matrix") or []
    if not models:
        return ""
    lines = [
        "\\section{Inter-model agreement (binary contradicting label)}",
        "\\begin{tabular}{l " + " ".join(["r"] * len(models)) + "}",
        "\\toprule",
        " & " + " & ".join(_escape(m) for m in models) + " \\\\",
        "\\midrule",
    ]
    for i, m in enumerate(models):
        cells = [_fmt(matrix[i][j], digits=2) for j in range(len(models))]
        lines.append(f"\\texttt{{{_escape(m)}}} & " + " & ".join(cells) + " \\\\")
    lines += ["\\bottomrule", "\\end{tabular}", ""]
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _stat_test_section(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    t = analysis.get("stat_test", {}) or {}
    method = _escape(str(t.get("method", "n/a")))
    notes = _escape(str(t.get("notes", "")))
    return (
        "\\section{Statistical test: do models differ controlling for domain?}\n"
        f"\\textbf{{Method.}} \\texttt{{{method}}}\\\\\n"
        f"\\textbf{{Statistic.}} {_fmt(t.get('statistic'))}\\\\\n"
        f"\\textbf{{p-value.}} {_fmt(t.get('p_value'))}\\\\\n"
        f"\\textbf{{Observations.}} {int(t.get('n_observations', 0))} across "
        f"{int(t.get('n_models', 0))} models.\\\\\n"
        f"\\textit{{{notes}}}\n\n"
    )


def _figures_section(analysis: dict[str, Any], figures_dir: Path, tex_dir: Path) -> str:
    figs = analysis.get("figures") or {}
    out: list[str] = ["\\section{Figures}"]
    bars = figs.get("bars")
    ag = figs.get("agreement")
    # Convert paths to relative form for LaTeX includegraphics
    def _rel(path_like: Any) -> str | None:
        if not path_like:
            return None
        p = Path(path_like)
        if not p.exists():
            p = figures_dir / Path(path_like).name
            if not p.exists():
                return None
        try:
            return os.path.relpath(p, tex_dir)
        except ValueError:
            return str(p)

    bars_rel = _rel(bars)
    ag_rel = _rel(ag)
    if bars_rel:
        out.append(
            "\\begin{figure}[h]\\centering"
            f"\\includegraphics[width=0.95\\linewidth]{{{bars_rel}}}"
            "\\caption{Per-model accuracy by runner. The QH prediction is "
            "that \\texttt{contradiction\\_geometry} should beat \\texttt{cosine} "
            "on every back-end; deviations are evidence about the "
            "model-versus-language question.}"
            "\\end{figure}"
        )
    if ag_rel:
        out.append(
            "\\begin{figure}[h]\\centering"
            f"\\includegraphics[width=0.7\\linewidth]{{{ag_rel}}}"
            "\\caption{Inter-model agreement on the binary contradicting "
            "label, geometry runner. High off-diagonal entries indicate "
            "the geometry signal is consistent across embedding spaces.}"
            "\\end{figure}"
        )
    return "\n\n".join(out) + "\n"


def _losses_section(analysis: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    losses = analysis.get("geometry_losses") or []
    if not losses:
        return (
            "\\section{Honest negative finding}\nOn the models reported "
            "here the geometry probe does not lose to the cosine "
            "baseline on AUROC. This is not strong evidence for the "
            "hypothesis (the strongest test is the per-domain "
            "breakdown); it only means we have nothing to retract on "
            "this run.\n\n"
        )
    rows = "\n".join(
        f"\\texttt{{{_escape(r['model'])}}} & {_fmt(r['geometry_auroc'])} & "
        f"{_fmt(r['cosine_auroc'])} & {_fmt(r['delta'])} \\\\"
        for r in losses
    )
    return (
        "\\section{Honest negative finding}\n"
        "On the following models the firm's contradiction-geometry "
        "probe ties or loses to the cosine baseline on AUROC. This "
        "finding is reported in the abstract for the same reason: the "
        "firm's credibility on its methodological reorientation "
        "depends on publishing failures, not just successes.\n\n"
        "\\begin{tabular}{l r r r}\n"
        "\\toprule\n"
        "Model & Geometry AUROC & Cosine AUROC & $\\Delta$ (cosine $-$ geometry) \\\\\n"
        "\\midrule\n"
        f"{rows}\n"
        "\\bottomrule\n"
        "\\end{tabular}\n\n"
    )


def render_tex(analysis: dict[str, Any], figures_dir: Path, tex_dir: Path) -> str:
    today = dt.date.today().isoformat()
    parts: list[str] = []
    parts.append(_TEX_HEADER % {"today": today})
    parts.append(_abstract(analysis))
    parts.append(
        "\\section{Background}\nThe Quintin Hypothesis (QH) predicts "
        "that the difference vector between embeddings of a premise "
        "and its logical contradiction is sparse (concentrated in few "
        "dimensions, measured by Hoyer sparsity) while the difference "
        "between a premise and a coherent continuation is dense. If "
        "this is a property of language, the prediction should hold "
        "across embedding back-ends. If it is a property of one "
        "specific model — for instance \\texttt{text-embedding-3-large} "
        "— that is also useful information, but it is a much weaker "
        "claim than the firm originally asserted.\n\n"
    )
    parts.append(_losses_section(analysis))
    parts.append(_per_model_table(analysis))
    parts.append("\n\n")
    parts.append(_per_domain_table(analysis))
    parts.append("\n\n")
    parts.append(_agreement_table(analysis))
    parts.append("\n\n")
    parts.append(_stat_test_section(analysis))
    parts.append(_figures_section(analysis, figures_dir, tex_dir))
    parts.append(
        "\\section{Reproducibility}\n"
        "The benchmark dataset is frozen as \\texttt{qh-v1} under "
        "\\texttt{benchmarks/quintin\\_hypothesis/v1/}. Per-model "
        "predictions are persisted as parquet under "
        "\\texttt{results/cross\\_model/}; raw embedding vectors are "
        "stored off-tree (default \\texttt{\\textasciitilde/.theseus/data/cross\\_model/}) "
        "with a manifest. The figures and numbers in this PDF are "
        "regenerated from \\texttt{cross\\_model\\_analysis.json} on each "
        "build; no number is hand-edited.\n\n"
    )
    parts.append("\\end{document}\n")
    return "\n".join(parts)


def compile_pdf(tex_path: Path, pdf_path: Path) -> bool:
    """Compile the .tex with pdflatex twice, if it is available."""
    if not shutil.which("pdflatex"):
        print("pdflatex not installed; skipping PDF compile. Source: " + str(tex_path))
        # Drop a tiny placeholder PDF so artefact-publishing steps
        # don't fail. The .tex is the source of truth.
        if not pdf_path.exists():
            pdf_path.write_bytes(b"%PDF-1.4\n% placeholder, install pdflatex to render\n%%EOF\n")
        return False
    out_dir = tex_path.parent
    for _ in range(2):
        subprocess.run(
            [
                "pdflatex",
                "-interaction=nonstopmode",
                "-halt-on-error",
                "-output-directory",
                str(out_dir),
                tex_path.name,
            ],
            cwd=str(out_dir),
            check=True,
        )
    return True


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    p.add_argument("--analysis", required=True, type=Path)
    p.add_argument("--figures", required=True, type=Path)
    p.add_argument("--tex", required=True, type=Path)
    p.add_argument("--pdf", required=True, type=Path)
    args = p.parse_args(argv)

    if not args.analysis.exists():
        # Allow stub generation when there is no analysis yet — the .tex
        # carries an explanatory abstract so reviewers see the design.
        analysis: dict[str, Any] = {
            "n_rows": 0,
            "per_model": {},
            "per_domain": {},
            "agreement_models": [],
            "agreement_matrix": [],
            "stat_test": {
                "method": "no_data",
                "statistic": float("nan"),
                "p_value": float("nan"),
                "notes": "No analysis JSON found at build time.",
                "n_observations": 0,
                "n_models": 0,
            },
            "geometry_losses": [],
            "figures": {"bars": None, "agreement": None},
        }
    else:
        analysis = json.loads(args.analysis.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

    args.tex.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    tex_text = render_tex(analysis, args.figures, args.tex.parent)
    args.tex.write_text(tex_text, encoding="utf-8")
    print(f"wrote {args.tex}")

    args.pdf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    compile_pdf(args.tex, args.pdf)
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
